# Product Requirements Checklist: Weight Loss Calorie Tracker

**Purpose**: Validate the completeness, clarity, and consistency of the product requirements before task generation
**Created**: 2026-04-02
**Feature**: [/home/ae/weight-loss-app/specs/001-calorie-photo-tracker/spec.md](/home/ae/weight-loss-app/specs/001-calorie-photo-tracker/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the exact onboarding inputs required to calculate the daily calorie budget explicitly specified? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the spec define whether profile changes recalculate only future budgets or also alter historical summaries? [Gap, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK003 Are the minimum required outputs of a food-photo estimation defined beyond calories, such as confidence or ambiguity indicators? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec define the minimum contents of the 7-day and 30-day summaries beyond "simple stats"? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements for entry editing and deletion sufficiently documented for both today's totals and past-period trend totals? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "minimum personal details" quantified clearly enough to avoid different onboarding implementations? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "recognized calorie-needs formula" specific enough to keep budget calculation behavior consistent across implementations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "silently by default" defined with observable product boundaries, including what user confirmation or review steps are still allowed? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "simple summary view" defined with measurable display expectations rather than relying on subjective interpretation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "limited confidence" defined with criteria that can be applied consistently to estimation results and history views? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the requirements for silent logging align with the separate requirement that users can edit or delete incorrect entries without conflict? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK012 Do the success criteria for meal logging speed align with any implied need for user review of uncertain AI estimates? [Consistency, Spec §SC-002, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK013 Are the assumptions about a minimal feature set consistent with the plan's PWA-first, local-first scope and absence of backend services? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Summary, Plan §Technical Context]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK014 Can the acceptance scenarios for onboarding objectively distinguish between valid completion, invalid input, and repeat-open behavior? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK015 Can the acceptance scenarios for food-photo logging objectively distinguish between successful estimation, low-confidence estimation, and failed estimation? [Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK016 Are the trend-view success criteria measurable enough to determine what counts as adequate usability for 7-day and 30-day access? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005, Spec §User Story 4]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are primary requirements complete for all four core user flows: onboarding, photo logging, today's summary, and recent trends? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1-4]
- [ ] CHK018 Are alternate-flow requirements defined for users who want to correct an AI estimate before relying on it for daily totals? [Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK019 Are exception-flow requirements defined for unreadable photos, unavailable estimation service, or no estimation result? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Contracts §AI Estimation Service]
- [ ] CHK020 Are recovery-flow requirements defined for returning to a usable state after failed capture, failed estimation, or accidental entry deletion? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are date-boundary requirements specific enough to prevent inconsistent handling of entries logged around midnight or across local-day changes? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for partial-history windows when fewer than 7 or 30 days of data exist, including how totals and averages should be interpreted? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK023 Are multi-item or ambiguous meal-photo requirements documented well enough to determine expected fallback behavior? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-013]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK024 Are privacy and retention requirements defined for locally stored food photos and personal body data? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK025 Are offline-behavior requirements fully specified for which parts of the product must remain usable without network access? [Gap, Plan §Technical Context, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK026 Are accessibility requirements intentionally excluded or missing for mobile capture, summary reading, and trend interpretation? [Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK027 Is the assumption that a single-user, local-first model is sufficient validated against any future need for backup, restore, or device change? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK028 Are external dependency requirements for the AI estimation service documented at the requirements level, including availability expectations and failure impact? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §Summary, Contracts §AI Estimation Service]
- [ ] CHK029 Is the "not medical advice" assumption reflected clearly enough in user-facing requirement boundaries so the product cannot be mistaken for clinical guidance? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK030 Is a requirement and acceptance-criteria traceability scheme needed so future tasks can map implementation work directly to FRs, scenarios, and success criteria? [Traceability, Gap]
- [ ] CHK031 Do any requirements conflict between a minimal friction logging flow and the need to make uncertainty visible to the user? [Conflict, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK032 Are any critical product terms still subjective or underdefined, including "simple," "silently," "recognized," or "limited confidence"? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-013]

## Notes

- Audience: spec author
- Depth: standard
- Focus areas: full product requirements, especially AI logging ambiguity, daily aggregation rules, and trend-summary coverage
- User-specified must-haves incorporated: onboarding-based calorie budget, AI photo calorie estimation, today's spent vs remaining calories, and 7-day/30-day stats
